description: Client-side request forgery — postMessage hijacking, window.opener (reverse tabnabbing) chaining, fetch from window.name, BroadcastChannel abuse, SharedWorker leak. These are FRONTEND attacks driven by an attacker page or stored content; chain with XSS/CSRF/open-redirect for impact.
craft_guidance: 1. INVENTORY POSTMESSAGE: open DevTools, monkey-patch window.addEventListener('message', ...) on target. Note origins it accepts.
2. ORIGIN CHECK SLOPPINESS: many handlers do `e.origin.indexOf('target.com') !== -1` — bypass with 'target.com.evil.com' or 'evil.com?target.com'.
3. DATA SHAPE: send malformed JSON, undefined fields, prototype-polluted objects. See if handler crashes or sets state from fields it shouldn't.
4. WILDCARD POSTMESSAGE: target sends `postMessage(secret, '*')` — attacker iframe/popup captures it.
5. WINDOW.OPENER: target opens external link without rel='noopener'. Attacker page does `opener.location='https://evil.com/phish'`.
6. WINDOW.NAME: persists across navigation. Attacker sets window.name in attacker domain, victim navigates to target which reads window.name as data.
7. BROADCASTCHANNEL: same-origin only, but abuse via subdomain takeover or stored XSS that broadcasts.
8. SHAREDWORKER LEAK: workers persist across tabs. If a tab leaks data via SharedWorker, attacker tab reads it.
9. CHAIN: postMessage hijack alone is rarely high — chain with token theft, account-action via authenticated fetch from victim's session.
10. CSP IMPACT: even strict CSP doesn't block postMessage. Don't assume 'CSP present = safe'.

postmessage_origin_bypass.description: Target's message handler has loose origin check; attacker frame passes the check and sends malicious data.

probes.description: Cross-origin iframe + postMessage with admin action. Test if handler validates origin (it should reject).

window_opener_attack.description: Target opens external link without rel='noopener noreferrer'. Attacker landing page redirects opener back to phishing clone.

probes.description: Confirm rel='noopener' missing. Check response HTML for `target='_blank'` without `rel='noopener'`.

wildcard_postmessage_send.description: Target sends sensitive data via postMessage with target origin '*'. Any iframe parent reads it.

probes.description: Search response JS for `postMessage(.*,\s*['\"]\*['\"]\)`. Confirm via browser_navigate + DevTools listener on opening parent.

broadcast_channel_leak.description: Same-origin BroadcastChannel — if any subdomain has stored XSS or is taken over, attacker reads/writes channel messages.

probes.description: Search response for `new BroadcastChannel(`. Channel name + handler logic in JS bundle reveals what data flows.
